data.title: Hecla Mining Q4 earnings beat estimates by 25% with EPS of $0.2. Revenue up $198.46 million YoY.
data.description: Hecla Mining (NYSE: HL) revealed its fourth-quarter earnings on the afternoon of Tuesday, February 17, 2026. The key highlights from the financial report included surpassing estimated earnings by 25.0%, with an earnings-per-share (EPS) of $0.20 compared to an estimated $0.16. Revenue experienced a substantial increase of $198.46 million from the corresponding period in the previous year. Looking at the preceding quarter, Hecla Mining had also outperformed expectations. For a more comprehensive report, the full story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Kenvue Inc. (NYSE: KVUE) reported Q4 2025 and full-year results, achieving stronger top and bottom-line performance. CEO Kirk Perry optimistic for 2026.
data.description: In Summit, New Jersey, Kenvue Inc. (NYSE: KVUE) released their financial results for the fiscal fourth quarter and full year ended on December 28, 2025. CEO Kirk Perry stated that they concluded 2025 with improved top and bottom-line performance in the fourth quarter due to disciplined execution and a more advantageous year-ago sales comparison. As they move into 2026, they are concentrating on further enhancing their performance.

data.title: NVIDIA lands big AI deal with Meta for millions of Blackwell and Rubin GPUs, Grace CPUs, and Spectrum-X networking.
data.description: In a significant move, Meta has formally agreed to a multiyear partnership with NVIDIA, a renowned tech giant. This partnership will see Meta utilizing millions of GPUs, as well as Grace CPUs and Spectrum-X networking systems within their hyperscale AI data centers. The commitment to this collaboration underscores the scale and longevity of the partnership between the two companies. The deployment of these cutting-edge technologies aims to further enhance Meta's capabilities in the realm of artificial intelligence, indicating a substantial investment in this area.

data.title: TPG RE Finance Trust (TRTX) reports Q4 earnings, EPS of $0.24 versus $0.25 estimate, revenue up $302k from last year.
data.description: TPG RE Finance Trust (NYSE: TRTX) released its Q4 earnings on February 17, 2026, at 04:37 PM. The company fell short of estimated earnings by -4.0%, posting an EPS of $0.24 compared to the expected $0.25. Revenue saw an increase of $302,000 from the previous year. In the prior earnings period, the company had missed expectations. For more details, the full report is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Innospec's Q4 revenue: $455.6M, 7% growth in Fuel Specialties, net income $47.4M, EPS $1.91. Full year revenue: $1.78B, $116.6M net income, GAAP EPS $4.67.
data.description: Innospec Inc. reported its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025. The company generated total revenues of $455.6 million in Q4, a 2% decrease from the previous year. Net income for the quarter was $47.4 million, with GAAP EPS at $1.91 and adjusted non-GAAP EPS at $1.50. For the full year, total revenues were $1.78 billion, a 4% decrease from the prior year, with net income of $116.6 million and adjusted non-GAAP EPS of $5.27. Innospec generated an excellent quarterly cash flow of $61.4 million and closed the year with net cash of $292.5 million. The company showed continued strength in Fuel Specialties with 7% operating income growth, while Performance Chemicals and Oilfield Services also demonstrated sequential improvement. The company's CEO expressed optimism for further improvements in 2026 and highlighted various strategic initiatives. Performance Chemicals had revenues of $168.4 million in Q4, consistent with the previous year, while Fuel Specialties saw revenues of $194.1 million, up 1% from the previous year. Oilfield Services generated revenues of $93.1 million, a 12% decrease from the previous year. Corporate costs decreased by $4.6 million, and the company had a net cash balance of $292.5 million as of December 31, 2025. Innospec will focus on margin and operating income improvement in Performance Chemicals and Oilfield Services in 2026.

data.title: Advocates in Florida Capitol rally to protect HIV drug access for thousands in need of lifesaving treatment.
data.description: In Tallahassee, Florida, a recent rally at the state Capitol saw advocates coming together to emphasize the importance of ensuring continued access to a crucial HIV medication for numerous individuals. The event aimed to protect the lifesaving drug access for thousands of people in the state.

data.title: Bridge, owned by Stripe, receives conditional OCC approval for national bank charter to offer stablecoin services publicly.
data.description: Pending final approval, the regulatory authority is considering allowing Bridge to provide services related to stablecoins and digital assets for businesses.

data.title: Ball Corporation to participate in Bank of America's 2026 Global Agriculture and Materials Conference. Leading provider of sustainable aluminum packaging. (Character count: 129)
data.description: A global provider of sustainable aluminum packaging, Ball Corporation (NYSE: BALL), based in Westminster, Colo., is set to participate in the Bank of America 2026 Global Agriculture and Materials Conference on February 17. With a focus on beverage, personal, and home care products, Ball Corporation is a leading player in the industry. This event offers a platform for the corporation to showcase its expertise in sustainable packaging solutions.

data.title: Chemours declares $0.0875 per share quarterly dividend for Q1 2026. Board of Directors announced the dividend.
data.description: In Wilmington, Delaware on February 17, 2026, the chemical company Chemours (NYSE: CC) revealed that its Board of Directors has approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.0875 per share for the first quarter of 2026 on its common stock.

data.title: Simmons First National announces $175M common stock repurchase program authorized by the Board of Directors.
data.description: Simmons First National Corporation (NASDAQ: SFNC) headquartered in Pine Bluff, Arkansas, revealed on February 17, 2026, that their Board of Directors has given the green light for a fresh stock repurchase initiative. This program will amount to $175 million and is aimed at bolstering the market value of the company's shares. Simmons' latest move signifies their confidence in the company's financial standing and a commitment to maximizing shareholder value. This authorization emphasizes the company's strong position in the market.

data.title: Empire State Realty Trust (NYSE: ESRT) reports Q4 and full year 2025 results for its NYC-focused REIT. Flagship Empire State Building named top attraction in NYC for fourth year.
data.description: Empire State Realty Trust, Inc. (NYSE: ESRT) is a Real Estate Investment Trust based in NYC. The company owns and manages a collection of office, retail, and multifamily properties in the city. Its most prominent asset is the Empire State Building, renowned as the "World's Most Famous Building". The building's Observatory, a top tourist destination, was recently recognized as the #1 attraction in NYC for the fourth year in a row in Tripadvisor's 2025 Travelers’ Choice Awards. ESRT's portfolio consists of well-leased, modernized, and strategically located assets.
